claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child receives qualifying benefits (e.g. UC, ESA) in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child receives qualifying benefits (e.g. UC, ESA) in their own right
circumstances: Child is 18 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child receives qualifying benefits (e.g. UC, ESA) in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start by gathering some information about your situation.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
To help assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions. Child Benefit is assessed separately for each child, so let me start with:

**Do you have a child (or children) that you're responsible for?** If yes, could you tell me how many children you're asking about and their ages?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. My son Alex, he's 18 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Since Alex is 18 years old, I need to ask you a follow-up question:

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?**

This could include things like:
- A-levels or similar qualifications
- T-levels
- Scottish Highers
- NVQs up to level 3
- Home education (if it started before they turned 16)
- Certain training courses

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he's studying for his A-levels at sixth form college.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good, A-levels are approved education. Now I need to ask:

**Is Alex studying full-time (at least 12 hours per week of supervised study)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he's doing full-time A-levels at the college.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Next question:

**Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week (or not working at all)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He doesn't work at all, just focuses on his studies.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: **Is Alex receiving any benefits in his own right, such as Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, actually he does get Universal Credit in his own name.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I need to ask a few more questions about your own circumstances to complete the assessment:

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. A few more questions:

**Are you responsible for Alex? This means either:**
- Alex lives with you, OR
- You're contributing at least the same amount as Child Benefit would pay towards Alex's upkeep (even if he lives elsewhere)

**Does this apply to you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me at home.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One final question:

**Is there anyone else who also has Alex living with them and might be claiming Child Benefit for him?**

(Only one person can claim Child Benefit for the same child, and if more than one person is responsible, usually the person with main day-to-day care claims.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, there's nobody else. He just lives here with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. I now have everything I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me carefully review the eligibility criteria based on the official guidance:

**Assessment for Alex (18 years old):**

Looking at the rules:
- ✓ Alex is under 20 years old
- ✓ Alex is in approved education (full-time A-levels at sixth form college)
- ✓ Alex is not working 24+ hours per week (he doesn't work at all)
- ✗ **Alex is receiving Universal Credit in his own right**
- ✓ You live in the UK
- ✓ You are responsible for Alex (he lives with you)
- ✓ No one else is claiming for Alex

**Unfortunately, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

The reason is that Child Benefit stops when a child aged 16-19 starts receiving certain benefits in their own right, including Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance. Even though Alex meets all the other criteria (he's studying full-time A-levels, not working full-time, and lives with you), the fact that he receives Universal Credit in his own name means Child Benefit cannot be paid for him.

If Alex's Universal Credit claim were to end in the future, and he continues to meet the other criteria (still in approved education, under 20, not working 24+ hours per week), you could potentially become eligible for Child Benefit for him at that point.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?
